title: ProjectEntity (base contract)
description: The PROJECT-AGNOSTIC core of a planning artifact's frontmatter: the three primitives (Identity, State, Relation) and nothing project-specific. The set of kinds, the per-kind status enums, the body-section conventions, the tag registry, the milestone markers, and the rel->kind target rules all live in a project-system.config.json (see project-config.schema.json) — NOT here. The validator composes this base with a project's config to form the effective contract. Documented by docs/spec/schema.md.
x-configRequired: Per-project specifics (kinds, status enums, sections, tagRegistry, milestones, relTargetKinds, render metadata) are supplied by a project-system.config.json validated against project-config.schema.json. This base file is identical across every consuming project; it is the artifact a consumer mirrors and the drift check compares against.

kind.description: Identity — the discriminant. Populated by the loader from the folder name; NEVER hand-authored. The allowed values are the project's declared kinds (config.kinds), not fixed here.

id.description: Identity — the filename stem. Populated by the loader from the filename; NEVER hand-authored. The link target.

title.description: Identity — human label. The only authored Identity field.

status.description: State — a string at the base; narrowed to a per-kind enum by the project config (config.kinds[kind].status).

updated.description: State — ISO date of the last substantive edit. The validator additionally rejects future dates.

links.description: Relation — typed edges; omit if none.

tags.description: Accidental facets. The known-key registry and value linting are project config; the base only forbids a tag from shadowing a primitive.

rel.description: The universal planning-edge vocabulary. A project may map each rel to the kinds it may target (config.relTargetKinds) but does not redefine the vocabulary here.

target.description: '<kind-folder>/<stem>' (e.g. 'decisions/0009-unified-registry'), a milestone marker (e.g. 'M5'), or an external ref ('slice.md#M5'). The folder set and milestone vocabulary are project config.
